message:
fix(handoff): Prevent false positive login detection with negative patterns

Add negative pattern matching to IsLoginComplete() methods to prevent
falsely detecting login success when output actually indicates failure.
Without this check, messages like "ensure you are logged in" or "not
logged in" could trigger false positive login completion detection.

Changes in handoff/claude.go and handoff/gemini.go:
- Check for negative patterns FIRST before checking success patterns
- Negative patterns: "not logged in", "failed to log in", "login failed",
  "unable to log in", "ensure you are logged in", "make sure you are
  logged in", plus "not authenticated" for Gemini
- Return false immediately if any negative pattern matches

Changes in coordinator/state.go (DetectState priority reorder):
- Check LoginSuccess FIRST (highest priority - if we're logged in, that
  matters most for determining next action)
- Check LoginFailed second (explicit failure state)
- Check OAuth URL patterns (implies awaiting URL state)
- Check method selection prompt
- Check RateLimit LAST (lowest priority - rate limit messages may persist
  in scrollback buffer even after successful recovery)

This prevents state machine confusion when rate limit messages from prior
sessions are still visible in the terminal output during login flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
